title: pilosella aurantiaca subsp. dimorpha
intro: Pilosella aurantiaca subsp. dimorpha, also known as a subspecies of the Orange Hawk's-beard plant, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Pilosella aurantiaca subsp. dimorpha can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional European medicine to promote overall well-being.
